comments.author: reader
comments.text: Correct. The board should be a totally self-contained widget. Apart from the SPECIAL CASE of dragging from the hand to the board, everything happens in the board widget, and how it's positioned within the broader app needs to be completely flexible.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Correct. The transformation here should be simple here, too. When the hand card is still in the hand, compute viewport-relative starting point. When it lands on the board, transform viewport-relative endpoint to board-relative endpoint. Once the cards lands on the board, we should be using board-relative coords EVERYWHERE.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: This sounds WAY too complicated. You can position the hand and the board using any mechanism you want. When you need to use viewport coordinates, just ask the browser to translate within-element positions to viewpoint coordinates or vice versa.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Yes, and that's fine. Just know that when you save off board-to-board moves, you can recompute the array of path coordinates to be relative to the board. It's simple code. Once you do that map, ask yourself what value do the original viewport coordinates have for any future consumers. Spoiler: NONE. Just make sure you save the explicit fact that the frame-of-reference was within-board. Enum: VIEWPORT_PATH, BOARD_PATH.
